description: Capability policy for the Hermes ↔ frontier-os operator bridge. Hermes (or any external operator surface) is allowed to invoke frontier-os via these wrapper scripts ONLY. Mutating verbs are gated and require explicit operator approval through the chat surface; the wrapper refuses unless the caller passes a fresh approval token.

allowed.rationale: Read-only by contract — assesses lock + latest-run + kill-switch and returns a status code.
Shadow runs DO write latest-run + lease + ledger entries, but never emit factory alerts and never touch launchd. Safe-by-default for operator chat.
Observe mode runs no primary or repair (reconciler invariant I13).

allowed.underlying: git diff + node --test + frontier factory reconcile --mode shadow
allowed.rationale: Aggregates evidence for a PR review. May trigger a shadow reconcile (already safe). Never pushes branches or comments on PRs.
Prints proposed plist + backup path + rollback command. Pure read.

gated.rationale: Active mode emits real factory alerts. Requires operator approval for the current session.
Writes a real plist on disk + mode.json. Needs explicit operator approval token. The script still refuses to call launchctl itself — the operator runs the reload.

blocked.rationale: AGENTS.md hard rule #2 — the operator runs launchctl, never the bridge.
AGENTS.md hard rule #3 — the upstream lane wrapper at /Users/test/bin is owned by the operator.
Hermes never pushes branches. Pushes are operator-driven.
Hermes never commits. Commits are operator-driven.
No destructive shell verbs through the bridge.
The ledger is append-only via frontier CLI; Hermes never touches the SQLite file directly.

notes: The bridge is a one-way street: Hermes → frontier-os. Frontier-os does NOT call back into Hermes from the bridge.
Approval tokens are minted by the operator out-of-band (chat reply, signed message, etc.) and consumed once. The wrapper scripts refuse if the token's scope doesn't match the verb.
Adding a verb requires updating tests/hermes/policy.test.ts in the same commit.
